title: Why was Gendou having sex with the Akagis
display_title: Why was Gendou having sex with the Akagis
wikitext:
[]: Why was Gendou having sex with the Akagis?             Quote Post by chaosakita » Wed Dec 23, 2009 12:05 am From my other thread, the Morality of Gendou: Obsessive Maths Freak wrote: Episode #21' wrote: AKAGI (OFF) : Are you really sure? IKARI : Yes, I have no regrets concerning my work. AKAGI : Liar! You just can't forget about Ms. Yui, right? But I don't mind.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Wed Dec 23, 2009 12:06 am Why was Gendou having sex with the Akagis?

[]:             Quote Post by Bomby von Bombsville » Wed Dec 23, 2009 12:38 am Explanation: He has a penis. Sometimes a man wants sex. His wife is gone, and someone else was available and willing. He was probably fantasizing about Yui when he was banging them anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Wed Dec 23, 2009 12:39 am esselfortium wrote: Well, come on; let's be honest here...who wouldn't ?

[]:             Quote Post by chaosakita » Wed Dec 23, 2009 12:41 am ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: I would encourage people to move away from the clichéd view of Gendou as the "Uber-Pimp" and to be mindful of the general rule of strong-willed women in Evangelion. It is more likely that the real question in this thread should be why the Akagi's were having sex with Gendou.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Dec 23, 2009 12:49 am Because it gave him an extra degree of control over them, making his elaborate scheme possible. Also because Yui was unavailable and he has a lab coat fetish.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Dec 23, 2009 1:23 am Pretty much what Nemz said.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Wed Dec 23, 2009 1:55 am I think he slept with them because they wanted him to. They both persuaded a relationship with him; so naturally sleeping with him would be part of that. I don’t think Gendo cared about it much one way or the other, probably just viewed sleeping with them as something he had to do. Just another step on the road he had chosen to take.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Wed Dec 23, 2009 10:17 am Some things to consider: 1) Gendou is an extremely lonely person. All the evidence is that Yui was the only person who ever really loved him, and despite his claim to Fuyutsuki in 21 that he's okay with that, I doubt he is. Very few people are happy being unloved. Gendou does what Misato does - figures that sex is a good substitute for actual emotional contact. 2) Naoko looks a lot like Yui. (Yes, yes, it's anime and everyone looks the same, I know). Both have short brown hair and green eyes. They're both also brilliant scientists who Gendou considers to be on his level intellectually. For that matter, Ritsuko looked a lot like Naoko (and by extension Yui) until she dyed her hair. 3) Gendou is not as strong-willed as he pretends/wants to be. If Naoko propositioned him repeatedly - and to some degree it seems like she did - I doubt he'd have the guts to turn her down every time. He even complains about her being pushy and forcing him into a relationship. 4) Gendou is considered to be rather attractive. He's got that dark mysterious stranger thing going on, and let's face it, even with the überpimp stuff aside, he's a pretty cool guy. It seems to me like the relationship with Naoko was begun by Naoko, rather against Gendou's wishes, and he just didn't have the courage - or didn't care enough - to break it off. As for the younger generation: it's clear from 21 and other episodes that Ritsuko, too, was carrying some sort of torch for Gendou, and it's not too hard to guess that she started the relationship, rather than Gendou. And again, Gendou either didn't care enough to say no or didn't want to jeopardize their working relationship. Office relationships are bad enough when going well in a normal setting. When both people in the relationship are high-ranking officers in an institute which quite literally has the power to destroy the entire human race, it may be better not to risk a bad break-up.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed Dec 23, 2009 7:19 pm Good points made all around about why Gendo slept with the Akagis. ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: It is more likely that the real question in this thread should be why the Akagi's were having sex with Gendou.

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Wed Dec 23, 2009 7:29 pm What women would not want to jump on Ikari?

[]:             Quote Post by chaosakita » Wed Dec 23, 2009 9:57 pm Otakon 08 Ikari wrote: What women would not want to jump on Ikari?

[]:             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Wed Dec 23, 2009 10:13 pm Non-serious answer: Gendo has a penis, is a straight male, and has an attractive woman that wants to sleep with him. Do the math. Serious Answer:Gendo was single, and needed Naoko and Ritsuko to work on the Eva project. The fact that they were willing to sleep with him only sweetened the deal for him.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Dec 23, 2009 10:47 pm Gendo and Shinji suffer from the same disposition: They simply agree to things to get by in life. Never did Gendo start a relationship with Naoko or Ritsuko Akagi. He merely went along with it. In a similar fashion, Shinji never agreed to pilot the Evangelion, he merely went along with it. Unlike Gendo, Shinji realized later (Episode 19, Introjection ) that he needed to pilot the Eva and initiated it himself . Whereas Shinji chose willingly, Gendo simply killed Ritsuko, and ran away from that particular problem ( End of Evangelion , Air ).

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed Dec 23, 2009 11:00 pm Gendo is much more of a go-getter than Shinji. It was Gendo that proposed the HIP and it was Gendo that went behind Seele's back to engineer his own version.

[]:             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Wed Dec 23, 2009 11:07 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: Gendo is much more of a go-getter than Shinji. It was Gendo that proposed the HIP and it was Gendo that went behind Seele's back to engineer his own version.

[]:             Quote Post by chaosakita » Wed Dec 23, 2009 11:49 pm CyberXIII wrote: I really hate to say this, but saying that Gendo is more of a go-getter than Shinji is like saying a star is a little brighter than a flashlight.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Dec 24, 2009 12:44 am I'll clarify the analogy: It's like saying tap water is a little less cold than fucking ice cubes.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu Dec 24, 2009 12:49 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: Gendo is much more of a go-getter than Shinji. It was Gendo that proposed the HIP and it was Gendo that went behind Seele's back to engineer his own version.

[]:             Quote Post by AVman9 » Thu Dec 24, 2009 2:23 am He wants to give them every reason to stay around. If that means having sex with them, so be it. The Magi are a key part of NERV operations, and only the Ikagis possess the knowledge of their inner-workings and programming. Plus he's kind of a selfish asshole.

IKARI : Yes, I have no regrets concerning my work. AKAGI : Liar! You just can't forget about Ms. Yui, right? But I don't mind.
